title: Doctor i18n migration — skill/onboard/cite batch (L4836-5170, ~29 sites)
description: Final doctor.ts migration batch: covers session_hints_stale, stale_serve_lock, relevance_fields_missing, skill_md_yaml, onboard_coverage, narrow_too_few (telemetry arm), and cite_coverage multi-arm checks. Lint #26-#29 + cite-coverage from rc.20. This task closes the in-file migration — after this, every `okCheck()` / `issueCheck()` in doctor.ts uses the translator.

files.target: L4836-5170 (approximately 29 okCheck/issueCheck call sites). Specific anchors: session_hints_stale L4836-4890, stale_serve_lock L4870-4890, relevance_fields_missing near narrow_too_few, skill_md_yaml_invalid L4890+, onboard_coverage near L5125, cite_coverage arms L5125-5170
files.change: Same migration pattern as TASK-02/03. After this batch, run `grep -c '"\(Bootstrap\|Knowledge\|Event\|Agents\|Stable\|Serve\|Skill\|Onboard\)' packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts | grep -v 't(' ` to verify zero hardcoded English check names remain. The 84 sites should all use t().

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L4836-5200 to enumerate remaining sites.
Apply identical migration pattern: name → t('doctor.check.X.name'), message → t('doctor.check.X.<arm>.message', vars), remediation → t('doctor.check.X.<arm>.remediation').

cite_coverage subcommand (rc.20) has more complex structure — multiple arms covering justActivated / perClient / dismissedReasons / noneReasons. Inspect the existing doctor.cite.* keys in en.ts (L152-200) — many keys already exist. Reuse them; do NOT create duplicates under doctor.check.cite_coverage.* namespace. The rc.26 task here is to ensure the cite-coverage rendering path uses t() consistently, not to add new keys for already-translated parts.
Skill_md_yaml_invalid has special list-rendering for affected files — translate the wrapper text, leave file paths as data.
Onboard_coverage emits a list of uncovered slots — same pattern: translate wrapper, keep slot names as data.
Final grep audit: `grep -nE 'okCheck\(|issueCheck\(' doctor.ts | grep -v 't('` → expect ZERO output (every call site references t).
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` → expect snapshot mismatches (TASK-05 finalizes).

reference.pattern: Continuation of TASK-02/03. cite_coverage reuses existing rc.20 doctor.cite.* keys.
reference.examples: rc.20 cite-coverage formatter already uses doctor.cite.* — extend the t() coverage to the wrapper functions in this batch.

rationale.chosen_approach: Final batch closes the in-file migration. Wave 2 ends here.
alternatives_considered: Skip cite_coverage in this RC (it's already partially translated) — rejected: leaving 50% migrated is worse than 100%; the wrapper functions use t() inconsistently and the rc.26 audit needs to be 100% clean
decision_factors: Wave 2 completion requires zero hardcoded English in doctor.ts
rationale.tradeoffs: cite_coverage's existing doctor.cite.* keys may need an audit — handled inline in implementation.

manual_checks: Final grep audit: zero hardcoded English check names in doctor.ts
success_metrics: After this task, `grep -nE 'okCheck\(|issueCheck\(' doctor.ts | grep -v 't('` returns 0 lines

criteria: Across the full doctor.ts file, `grep -cE 'okCheck\(|issueCheck\(' packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts` minus `grep -cE 'okCheck\(t\(|issueCheck\(t\(|, t\(' packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts` returns 0 (no untranslated call sites — final audit)
packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts grep -c '"All relevance_paths\|"No CLAUDE.md\|"Skipped"' (sample hardcoded English) returns 0
Commit msg: 'feat(rc26): doctor i18n batch 3 — skill/onboard/cite (TASK-04)'
